samples.xml_description:
<module id="RAM">
    <description>Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits. The RAM should be initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Read address.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Read data.</output>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Write address.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Write data.</input>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="WIDTH" value="6">Width of each memory location.</enum>
        <enum id="DEPTH" value="8">Number of memory locations.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <para>Create a RAM array with a width of WIDTH and a depth of DEPTH. Initialize all RAM positions with 0.</para>
        <para>Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock and the negative edge of the reset signal.</para>
        <para>On reset, clear the RAM array.</para>
        <para>Check if the write enable signal is active. If so, write the input data to the RAM at the specified address.</para>
        <para>Trigger the always block on the positive edge of the clock and the negative edge of the reset signal.</para>
        <para>On reset, clear the read data.</para>
        <para>Check if the read enable signal is active. If so, read the data from the RAM at the specified address into the read data output.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
